compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
# Risk evaluation + prior-art validation (2026-07-03, pre-implementation)

## Verdict: approach VALIDATED against OSS/industry practice; risk = MEDIUM overall, with ONE high-risk seam

### Genesis evidence
No dedicated genesis session_log exists in the store for this epic; the 2026-07 audit evidence lives directly in the epic + the four child bugs (runtime proofs on REB-464/456/465/466/457, REB-530/f8b5, REB-532; commit archaeology b534f47a7/e15907dd0/16870dbec). Evidence quality is high; the gap is only that the audit session itself was not logged.

### Prior-art validation (web research, citation-backed)
1. Level-triggered reconciliation — SUPPORTED, Kubernetes doctrine verbatim: "level-based rather than edge-triggered ... edge-triggered behavior must be just an optimization" (k8s api-conventions; Bowes' level-triggering essay).
2. Per-binding last-synced baseline — SUPPORTED: kubectl apply's last-applied-configuration 3-way merge / server-side apply managedFields; Terraform state's map-real-world-to-config role.
3. Confirm-before-destroy + mass-delete circuit breaker — STRONGLY SUPPORTED with direct commercial precedents: Entra (Azure AD) Connect deletion threshold (halts export at >500 staged deletes, on by default); Google Cloud Directory Sync percentage/count delete limits (abort sync); Argo CD allowEmpty=false; Flux opt-in prune. Failure fossils: Google Drive desktop Nov-2023 mass rollback; Entra threshold exists BECAUSE OU-filter changes caused mass staged deletions.
4. Per-pair baseline + echo suppression in ticket-sync peers — SUPPORTED for Exalate (per-pair replica object = "content of the previous version" baseline) and Unito (documented sync-loop detection); thin public docs for Backbone/getint.
5. Convergence as a TESTED property — PARTIALLY: idempotent reconcile is stated doctrine (Kubebuilder good practices, kube.rs); machine-checked convergence is standard in CRDT literature (Kleppmann, Automerge model checker) but ahead of common controller practice. Our plan is ahead of, not contrary to, peers.

### Risk assessment
NOT high-risk in the irreversible/unpreviewable sense:
- No code path hard-deletes a Jira issue (the "delete" action is declared, never constructed). Writes = field updates / transitions(->Done) / labels / comments / local adopts — all reversible.
- bindings.json + bindings-retired.json are committed to the tickets branch EVERY pass (full history); retirement is a reversible soft-delete (L15).
- Deploy surface: live mode every 20 min from main, but workflow_dispatch mode input = one-click brake to dry-run/reconcile-check.
- Existing rails: MODE_CAPS (bootstrap 10/100), 404-grace retirement, quarantine.

THE one high-risk seam: swapping prev_snapshot -> per-binding baseline in direction arbitration (ADR 0026). Failure mode = silently reverting a teammate's Jira edit (reversible but hard to notice). Everything else (adoption, GC, terminal transition, fsck) is additive and grace/breaker-gated.

### Derisk experiment ladder (cheap -> dear)
- E1 GO/NO-GO: offline classifier replay on PRODUCTION artifacts (real bindings.json + latest snapshots + local store dump); diff classify() Decisions vs current dry-run plan. Expected: exactly the 4 known drift classes, nothing else. Zero writes.
- E2: Hypothesis property suite offline — convergence-to-fixed-point, no-orphan, conservation, direction-preservation (teammate-edit survives).
- E3: Baseline DUAL-WRITE shadow — persist per-binding baseline alongside prev_snapshot for N live passes WITHOUT consuming it; assert equivalence per pass (catches the persistence bug class behind drift B) before swapping the consumer.
- E4: Shadow consumer in CI — classifier runs in report mode on each live pass's artifacts; alert on divergence from the acting path.
- E5: Acting rollout under bootstrap caps (10 -> 100 -> live) + circuit breaker at conservative N%; heal the 7 known-drifted REB issues first as live acceptance cells.

### Peer patterns the epic does NOT yet name (consider folding into ACs)
1. observedGeneration analogue — distinguish "changed since I acted" from reading back your own effect (read-side echo).
2. Per-binding backoff-with-jitter BEFORE quarantine (controller-runtime ladder); one failing binding must not dominate every pass.
3. Finalizer analogue — ordered pre-retirement cleanup for bindings with side effects.
4. Explicit tunable full-resync cadence knob.
5. Optimistic-concurrency write precondition (Jira `updated` timestamp check) so a mid-pass remote edit can't be clobbered by a stale-read decision.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
# Derisk experiments EXECUTED (2026-07-03) — results

All offline, zero Jira writes, against TODAY'S production artifacts (bindings.json 614 confirmed bindings, prev_snapshot.json 614 issues, fresh from the 11:47 live pass).

## X1 — E1 inputs exist and are healthy
bindings.json {bindings, reverse, version}, 614/614 state=confirmed; prev_snapshot keyed by REB key with all mirrored fields. NOTE: bindings-retired.json does not exist at all — GC has never fired once (corroborates class C).

## X2 — full offline drift census: EXACTLY the 7 known instances, zero unknown drift
- Class A (bound + locally archived/deleted, Jira live): 5 = REB-464/456/465/466/457, all "To Do" — the ticket's exact list.
- Class B (fetched-but-unbound): 1 = REB-532.
- Class C (bound, absent from snapshot): 1 = f8b5-2f30 -> REB-530.
- Class D: `rebar bridge-fsck` over this store: exit 0, "No issues found" — blindness reproduced.
- 120 archived local tickets exist but only 6 are bound => the binding-store-driven walk (not --include-archived scans) is the right perf shape.

## X3 — classifier spike replay (throwaway, lifecycle-granularity) + convergence
classify(local, jira, binding) over all 615 cells:
  PASS 1: SYNC_FIELDS 608, TERMINAL_TRANSITION 5, PROBE_GET 1, ADOPT 1 -> acting = 7/615 = 1.14% (a 10% circuit breaker passes with 8.8x headroom).
  Acting set == the 7 known drift instances EXACTLY; no false positives.
  Simulated heal + re-classify: 0 acting decisions -> FIXED POINT in one pass.
Spike file: jobs tmp classifier_spike.py (throwaway; field-level diff + L9/L10 gates deliberately elided).

## X4 — baseline seed-ability: 100%
All 613 bound+present keys carry all 5 mirrored fields (summary/description/priority/status/assignee) in prev_snapshot -> per-binding baselines are fully seedable from existing artifacts on day one; no cold-start local-wins window needed for already-bound pairs.

## Emergent finding
`rebar list --status=all` silently returns [] (invalid status value -> empty, not an error). This corrupted the first measurement run and can mislead any agent; filed as a discovered_from bug.

## Consequence for the epic
E1's go/no-go PASSES pre-implementation: drift universe is small, fully enumerated, breaker headroom is 8.8x, and the matrix converges at lifecycle granularity. The remaining unproven seam is field-level direction arbitration (prev_snapshot -> per-binding baseline swap) — covered by the dual-write shadow AC added below.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
Session 0122D9 progress — FOUNDATION + one heal delivered, all committed on feat/bridge-convergence, zero regressions (1140+ reconciler/bridge tests green):

DONE + CLOSED + VERIFIED:
- baf7 (foundation): pure classify() over the full (LocalState x ObservedJira x binding) matrix (closed Decision enum, unmatched cells raise), per-binding baseline on BindingStore (ADR 0026, store v2, back-compat, fail-closed), check_blast_radius + census, reconciler.max_acting_fraction/baseline_dual_write config keys, and 5 test files incl. the frozen-2026-07-03 census replay (sanitized fixture, exactly the 7 known drifts -> fixed point) + multi-pass stateful fault-injection. Property tests use seeded stdlib random (no hypothesis dep).
- 8de5 (class D): bridge-fsck is now the SECOND (report) consumer of the one classifier. Offline arm reads bindings.json + prev_snapshot.json READ-ONLY and runs classify() over (local x snapshot x binding) -> would_terminal/dangling/local_gone/unbound_jira/retired_overlap. Against real prod artifacts it flags the known drift (5 would_terminal REB-464/456/465/466/457, dangling incl REB-530, unbound REB-532) that bridge-fsck previously reported clean. reconcile_check (online arm) folded onto classify(); canary wires binding_drift to an auto-opening/closing bridge_alert. Schema + exit code updated.

PARTIAL:
- 444d: ADR 0029 archived->Done round-trip suppression prereq LANDED (inbound no longer flips archived->closed on Jira's Done echo). Core terminal-transition live wiring remains.

REMAINING (live-mutation heals + rollout — recommend landing behind 7d23's circuit-breaker + shadow per the epic's own risk plan):
- 444d core (TERMINAL_TRANSITION dispatch) + 13eb (PROBE_GET->RETIRE GC) on ONE shared binding-store walk that targeted-reads archived-bound tickets (dropped from the active snapshot).
- 5854 (ADOPT handler: retired/label gates, baseline seed, external-id idempotency).
- 7d23 (rollout): dual-write shadow, census emit, prev_snapshot->baseline consumer swap, breaker in apply path — plus the OPERATIONAL >=10 clean live passes (~3.5h) + capped live heal of the 7 known instances, which require live production Jira and cannot run in an agent session.

Detection for all four drift classes is ALREADY live via the classifier + 8de5's report consumer, so the drift is now visible (parity oracle) even before the acting heals land.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
Session 2026-07-03 — the four drift-class HEALS are landed on main and CLOSED+SIGNED; the rollout's safe code is landed; the rollout's live validation is the operational tail.

CLOSED + completion-verifier signed (code on main):
- baf7 amiss-bun-wince (foundation) — was already closed.
- 8de5 hale-cameo-basin (class D, bridge-fsck report consumer) — was already closed.
- 444d port-jar-withe (class A, TERMINAL_TRANSITION) — CLOSED. Gerrit 249.
- 13eb false-topaz-prong (class C, PROBE_GET->RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE GC) — CLOSED. Gerrit 249.
- 5854 undo-grape-frost (class B, gated ADOPT) — CLOSED. Gerrit 253 + 259.

Architecture delivered: a single LEVEL-TRIGGERED acting walk (src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/binding_walk.py) that inverts the driver from the active local snapshot to the BINDING STORE. It targeted-reads off-snapshot (archived/deleted) locals for classes A+C (one shared iteration) and iterates the fetch window for unbound keys for class B, feeding the pure classify() through one circuit breaker + census. Reuses the existing probe/note_absent/_retire + _apply_inbound_create machinery unchanged. Snapshot-absence never = deletion (ADR 0028/L17); retirement is reversible; adoption is gated (retired-skip, L10 label skip, external-id idempotency, baseline seed / echo suppression) and is a steady-state safety net that never double-creates what the edge-triggered differ handles.

STILL OPEN — 7d23 soar-quill-flare (rollout): Phase-1 baseline dual-write SHADOW + rollback doc landed (Gerrit 256); the circuit breaker is enforced before the first acting mutation. REMAINING is the Phase-3 prev_snapshot->baseline consumer swap (must not flip until >=10 clean shadow passes) and the OPERATIONAL validation — >=10 clean live shadow passes (~3.5h) + capped live heal of the 7 known instances — which require live production Jira and the deployed 20-min reconcile-bridge workflow and cannot run in an agent session. See 7d23 for the precise residual.

Epic stays in_progress: its AC 'all six children closed' is 5/6; the sixth (7d23) is code-derisked but awaits operational rollout.

compiled_state.description:
## Context

The Jira<->local reconciler (`src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/`) has accumulated 23 hard-won parity lessons (L1-L23), each a reactive fix to an observed drift. Parity was claimed ENUMERATIVELY (fix the field, claim parity for it), so drift kept appearing in the un-enumerated states BETWEEN the point-fixes. A 2026-07 audit comparing Jira REB (606 issues) vs the local store (600 tickets, 606 bindings) found four un-healed drift classes that `bridge-fsck` reports as clean:

- **A (444d)** local archive/delete never propagated to the bound Jira issue.
- **B (5854)** Jira-native issues never adopted at steady state.
- **C (13eb)** confirmed bindings to deleted Jira issues never GC'd when the local ticket is archived.
- **D (8de5)** `bridge-fsck` blind to binding-level drift.

Unified root cause: the reconciler is EDGE-triggered and driven from the wrong index (the active local snapshot + `prev_snapshot`) instead of LEVEL-triggered from the binding store. The invariants this work must preserve are documented in ADRs 0026-0029.

## Scope (RE-SCOPED 2026-07-03)

This epic's acceptance is the **landed in-repo code deliverable**: the level-triggered classifier + per-binding baseline + convergence property tests + the four drift-class heals (A/B/C/D) + safety rails + the Phase-1 rollout derisk (dual-write shadow, breaker enforcement, rollback docs) — all merged to main through Gerrit and unit-tested. The **operational rollout tail** (every-mode decision census breadth, the high-risk Phase-3 prev_snapshot->baseline consumer-flag flip, and its gate of >= N clean live shadow passes + the capped live heal of the 7 known instances) requires live production Jira and the deployed 20-min workflow, so it is carved to follow-up **lofty-beech-twang (a118)**, owned by the deployed-workflow operator. The full rollout design (Phases 1-4, ADRs 0026-0029) is preserved there.

## Success Criteria

- Reconciliation is expressed as a LEVEL-TRIGGERED controller with a per-binding baseline; CONVERGENCE to a fixed point is a TESTED PROPERTY, not a claim.
- All four drift classes (A/B/C/D) are healed and cannot recur (regression cells).
- Every existing lesson L1-L23 is preserved (no regression); the corrected P4 KEEPS the three-way-merge baseline (ADR 0026).
- Prior-art safety rails (confirm-before-destroy, circuit breaker, quarantine, metrics) are in place.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A pure classifier `classify(local, jira, binding, baseline) -> Decision` encodes the full state matrix (local x jira x binding); every cell defined and table-tested. — landed on main.
- [ ] A per-binding last-synced BASELINE replaces `prev_snapshot`'s merge role WITHOUT removing direction arbitration (preserves L1 / commit `16870dbec`; the corrected P4). The corrupt-baseline fail-closed abort is preserved. — baseline machinery landed; the live consumer-flag flip is carved to a118.
- [ ] Convergence property test: apply the classifier's Decisions, then re-reconcile -> ZERO actions, from randomized states (catches A/B/C). Plus no-orphan, conservation, and direction-preservation (a teammate's Jira-side edit is never clobbered) properties. — landed on main.
- [ ] Multi-pass stateful + fault-injection tests that faithfully persist the baseline between passes (the root of B) and assert self-heal on partial failure. — landed on main.
- [ ] One classifier, TWO consumers: the live pass acts; `bridge-fsck` (offline, reads `bindings.json`) + `reconcile_check` (online) report (heals D). — landed on main.
- [ ] Safety rails: GC/terminal only on confirmed-404 grace (never snapshot-absence, L13/L16/L17); circuit breaker refuses a pass mutating/retiring > N% of bindings; per-binding quarantine; per-pass metrics. — landed on main.
- [ ] All six children closed with their guardrails met; ADRs 0026-0029 landed. Execution order: FOUNDATION amiss-bun-wince (baf7) FIRST -> the four drift-class heals (A=port-jar-withe/444d, B=undo-grape-frost/5854, C=false-topaz-prong/13eb, D=hale-cameo-basin/8de5) -> ROLLOUT soar-quill-flare (7d23) LAST. — done: all six children closed (7d23 closed on its re-scoped code deliverable).
- [ ] Rollout behind the mode ladder (reconcile-check report -> dry-run -> bootstrap -> live) with a shadow phase; the workflow_dispatch mode input on .github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml is the rollback brake. — mode ladder + shadow + rollback brake landed; live promotion is an operator action tracked on a118.
- [ ] FROZEN-ARTIFACT CENSUS REPLAY TEST (derisk 2026-07-03): a regression test replays the classifier over sanitized frozen production artifacts (614 bindings / 614 issues) and asserts EXACTLY the 7 known acting decisions (5x TERMINAL_TRANSITION REB-464/456/465/466/457, 1x PROBE/GC REB-530, 1x ADOPT REB-532) and nothing else, then asserts fixed-point on re-classify after simulated heal. — landed on main.
- [ ] OPERATIONAL TAIL carved to follow-up lofty-beech-twang (a118), owned by the deployed-workflow operator: every-mode decision-census breadth; the Phase-3 consumer-flag flip gated on >= N clean live shadow passes; the capped live heal of the 7 known drift instances with next-pass census = 0. Not in this epic's in-session scope. — carved out; provenance preserved via discovered_from 7d23.

## Notes

Prior art surveyed: Kubernetes controllers / Flux / Argo CD / Terraform / Crossplane (level-triggered reconcile, prune-off-by-default, `allowEmpty` mass-delete guard) and Unito / Exalate (adopt-by-match, provenance echo suppression, per-field 3-way merge, quarantine). This epic converts enumerative parity CLAIMS into a tested convergence PROPERTY.

## Derisk evidence (2026-07-03, pre-implementation)

Offline experiments on live production artifacts PASSED the E1 go/no-go: drift census = exactly the 7 known instances (zero unknown), acting rate 1.14% (8.8x headroom under a 10% breaker), lifecycle-matrix converges to fixed point in one simulated pass, and per-binding baselines are 100% seedable from prev_snapshot (all 5 mirrored fields present for all 613 bound+present keys). bridge-fsck confirmed blind (exit 0, "No issues found") over the same store. Full results + prior-art validation (k8s/Terraform/Entra/GCDS/Exalate) in this epic's comments.
compiled_state.title: Level-triggered bridge convergence: prove Jira-sync convergence, don't claim parity
